Baldur’s Bones is a popular dice game in taverns. Each player requires several six-sided  dice. The rules are as follows:    Each player puts the agreed ante in the pot    Each player rolls three dice. Play then proceeds clockwise around the table, with the host  of the game going last    On their turn, a player can choose to "stand" or "roll." If the player stands, the next player  can take a turn. A player who rolls takes an additional die and rolls it. If the total of their  dice exceeds 21, they "bust" and are out of the game. Otherwise, they can keep rolling  additional dice until they either stand or break    After everyone has had a turn, the highest point total (excluding players who busted) wins  the game and takes the pot
Important NPCs
Strahd von Zarovich

Strahd von Zarovich is an imposing figure, exuding an aura of power and menace that  commands attention. He stands tall, his physique lean yet strong, a testament to his martial  prowess. His skin is pale, almost translucent, giving him an otherworldly appearance.    His face is sharp and angular, with high cheekbones and a pronounced jawline. His eyes are  perhaps his most striking feature - deep-set and crimson, they burn with an intense,  predatory gaze that seems to pierce one's very soul. His hair is jet black, slicked back from  his forehead, contrasting starkly with his pallid complexion.    Strahd is typically seen in opulent attire befitting his noble status. He favors dark colors,  often seen in a finely tailored black suit adorned with silver accents. Around his neck, he  wears a medallion bearing the crest of his family - a stylized dragon. His presence is further  accentuated by a flowing, crimson-lined black cape that billows dramatically behind him.    Despite his aristocratic appearance, there's an undeniable air of danger about Strahd. His  movements are precise and predatory, and his voice is a low, hypnotic drawl that can shift  from charming to terrifying in an instant. His mere presence is enough to send a chill down  anyone's spine, a chilling reminder of the power he wields.    Nezznar, the Black Spider
Session Notes:
Session 1: 11/7/23  Initial Notes for Session 1:
Focus on eerie nature. Everything is quiet. Fog coats the land. The whole world appears to be  holding its breath in anticipation.
1)​ The Black Spider received Strahd's letter, so when the party exits the cave, they find
the discarded letter and (once they get back to the main road) find carriage marks  and horse prints  2)​ When the party exits the cave, they find either a dead Gundren or a dying Gundren,

with his final request being that they transport his body back to Solario (which is  where you would meet the party)  3)​ The Black Spider is very pissed about you all killing their minions, sets up base at
Cragmaw Castle (or Strahd's Castle?) in order to have another go at cracking the  Forge  4)​ The party meets Madam Eva set up outside of Montera (maybe at the crossroads of
the Triboar Trail) and she entices the party to venture into Barovia to find the items  to seal away Strahd  5)​ If the party goes to Solario, Strahd could be there, laying down the law and
subjugating all who dare to refute his power. Essentially, Strahd will be imposing  himself as master of this land henceforth    Basic Session Plan:
-​ Leave cave  -​ Gundren’s body + letter  -​ To the road
-​ Carriage marks  -​ Solario
-​ City center where Strahd and The Black Spider are subjugated commoners  -​ Meet Lysandra at the edge of the center  -​ To Madam Eva (outside of Montera)    Run-notes for Session 1:

Session opener: End of WEC  After the end of WEC, the party discovered the body of Gundren who was conveniently on  his last breath as the party approached. He passed and asked them to bring his body to  Solaria. The party found The Black Spider’s letter, and decided to head to Montera.

In Montera, the party initially began by visiting Elsa, who asked the party to check on her  sister Gina Ironheart, who left Montera about a tenday ago and has not been seen for over  two tendays. She provided the party with hospitality, kindness, and pie. The party set up  watches throughout the night posted in the hallway outside of the rooms. Elincia went to  bathe in the river to the south of town, felt eyes on her, found a deer, and then headed back  inside.     After, the party went to Solario. When they got to the crossroads outside of Montera, they  saw cart tracks, see Triboar Trail. They fast-traveled to Solario from the crossroads,  arriving at the town square in the evening. There, Strahd was requesting the commoners to  swear fealty to him, or else there will be consequences. Beside him was Nezznar, the Black  Spider.     Viktor’s brother spoke up, requesting to go home. In response, Strahd lashed out with a  dagger and struck him dead with one brutal motion. This left Viktor hunched over his body,  sobbing with grief. Nala stood on the outside, shaking with anger, then the party grouped  up with her. Strahd announced a tithe of one tenth of every commoner's income. He then  left, with Nezznar following behind him. They boarded a black carriage helmed by spectral  horses and left.    From there, the party took Gundren’s body to his estate, where they met the estate keeper  Thoren. Thoren found the will of the Rockseekers, and the party brought the body to  Brother Ivan at St. Seraphine’s Cathedral. The Cathedral had been vandalized, and Ivan is  the only priest there. He requested the party grant the party more men to help defend the  cathedral. He took Gundren’s body from the party, and they went their separate ways.    From there, the party went back to the Rockseeker Estate to request that Thoren let them  stay the night there. Thoren politely declined them, and sent them to the Silverwater Inn.  The party went there, got some drinks, and played Baldur’s Bones with Captain Seraphine  and two of her guards. The party won some, and lost some, but had a good time. The party  was informed of Wights surveying the town and that going out at night is very dangerous.  This is where the session ended.    Session 2: 11/14/23

Pantheon
Genno, the True Form of Creator (True Neutral)

Physical Description: Genno appears as a colossal, ethereal figure, with a body made of  intertwining vines, trees, and elements of the natural world. Their face is obscured by a  luminous, shifting veil of leaves, symbolizing the ever-changing nature of creation. Genno's  arms are like massive tree branches that reach out to touch the world, imbuing it with life.  They emanate a serene, calming energy.    Explanation: Genno represents the impartial and balanced essence of creation. They exist  as the unifying force behind all life, emphasizing the interconnectedness of all living beings.  Genno's presence ensures that the world maintains equilibrium and harmony.    Gento, the Aspect of Civility (Lawful Good)

Physical Description: Gento takes on the form of a regal, golden-skinned humanoid figure  with luminous, benevolent eyes. They wear flowing robes adorned with intricate symbols  and are often depicted holding a book or scroll. A radiant aura surrounds them, casting a  warm and comforting light.    Explanation: Gento is the embodiment of civilization, enlightenment, and all that is  virtuous in society. They exist to inspire and guide mortals in their pursuit of knowledge,  justice, and diplomacy. Gento's presence encourages the development of culture, language,  and the betterment of the world.    Gresso, the Aspect of Deviation (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Gresso is a monstrous and twisted being with a body composed of  dark storm clouds and shadowy tendrils. Their eyes gleam with malevolent intent, and their  presence is accompanied by thunder and lightning. Gresso's form is ever-changing and  grotesque, reflecting the unpredictable nature of their domain.    Explanation: Gresso embodies the chaos and destruction inherent in the world. They exist  as a force of upheaval, driving the world towards change, sometimes for better but often  for worse. Gresso's influence gives rise to storms, calamities, and the creation of undead, as  they represent the unsettling aspects of existence.    Senzos, the True Form of Curiosity (Chaotic Good)
Physical Description: Senzos appears as a fiery and ever-changing entity, resembling a  swirling, multicolored tornado of flames. His eyes are bright, playful orbs, and he is often

depicted with a mischievous grin. His form is both ethereal and chaotic, with sparks and  embers dancing around him.    Explanation: Senzos embodies the spirit of unbridled curiosity and adventure. He  represents the desire to explore, learn, and embrace the unknown. Senzos is the patron of  creativity, inventiveness, and the burning passion to uncover new knowledge and  experiences.    Raggios, the Aspect of Knowledge (Chaotic Neutral)

Physical Description: Raggios is a figure of boundless energy and erratic motion. He  appears as a being composed of constantly shifting, ever-shifting geometric shapes and  patterns. His eyes are like swirling vortexes of information, and his form exudes a sense of  unpredictability and randomness.  Explanation: Raggios embodies the unpredictable and enigmatic aspects of knowledge. He  encourages exploration, experimentation, and thinking outside the box. Raggios represents  the pursuit of knowledge without constraints, even if it means embracing chaos and  ambiguity.    Celatos, the Aspect of Secrets (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Celatos is a shadowy figure, shrouded in darkness and cloaked in  secrecy. His eyes are concealed beneath a hood, and his presence is often accompanied by  eerie whispers. He moves silently and stealthily, leaving a sense of unease in his wake.    Explanation: Celatos embodies the darker side of curiosity, focusing on secrets, subterfuge,  and deceit. He thrives on hidden knowledge and the manipulation of truth. Celatos  represents the shadowy corners of existence where hidden agendas and concealed truths  lurk, making him both a source of intrigue and danger in the world.    Malignus, the True Form of Chaos (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Malignus takes on the form of an ever-shifting, monstrous entity,  constantly morphing between nightmarish forms. Their body is a swirling vortex of  darkness, and their eyes are chaotic voids that seem to devour all light. Malignus's presence  is disorienting, and they emit an unsettling, discordant energy.    Explanation: Malignus represents the raw and destructive force of chaos. They are the  source of disorder, turmoil, and malevolence in the world. Malignus's influence spawns  demons and other chaotic entities, sowing destruction and upheaval wherever they tread.
Licentus, the Aspect of Randomness (Chaotic Neutral)

Physical Description: Licentus appears as an unpredictable and enigmatic figure,  resembling a being of constantly shifting probability. They have a playful and mischievous  demeanor, with eyes that flicker with uncertainty. Their form seems to waver between  existence and non-existence, reflecting the unpredictable nature of chance.    Explanation: Licentus embodies the capriciousness of randomness and unpredictability.  They are the patron of chance, whimsy, and the unexpected. Licentus encourages  embracing the chaos of life's twists and turns, where fate is as likely to bring fortune as it is  to bring calamity.
Fortunus, the Aspect of Luck (Chaotic Good)

Physical Description: Fortunus takes on a charismatic and vibrant form, exuding an aura of  warmth and positivity. They have sparkling eyes that radiate joy and goodwill. Fortunus is  often depicted with an enchanting smile, bringing a sense of hope and serendipity to those  in their presence.    Explanation: Fortunus represents the brighter side of chaos, emphasizing good luck,  fortune, and serendipity. They are the embodiment of the belief that even in the midst of  chaos, opportunities for love and happiness can be found. Fortunus's influence encourages  acts of kindness, love, and spontaneous acts of goodwill.    Halosia, the True Form of Conquest (Lawful Evil)

Physical Description: Halosia appears as a towering, regal figure, draped in dark, imposing  armor adorned with symbols of dominance and control. Her eyes burn with an unyielding  ambition, and she wields a massive, sinister blade that glows with a malevolent energy.  Halosia's presence is authoritative and oppressive, exuding an aura of domination.    Explanation: Halosia is the personification of conquest, power, and control. She thrives on  the subjugation of others and seeks to expand her dominion relentlessly. Halosia's  influence is felt in the realms of death and among the devils, where her iron rule holds  sway.    Proelia, the Aspect of War (Neutral Evil)

Physical Description: Proelia is a fearsome and battle-hardened figure, with scars and  battle-worn armor that bear testament to countless conflicts. His eyes are cold and  calculating, and his presence exudes an aura of bloodlust and violence. Proelia wields a  jagged, brutal weapon, stained with the blood of foes.

Explanation: Proelia embodies the relentless and brutal aspects of war. He revels in  bloodshed, conflict, and the pursuit of power through force. Proelia's domain is marked by  aggression and warfare, as he seeks to claim victory and dominion over others through  martial prowess and cunning tactics.    Imperia, the True Form of Control (Lawful Good)

Physical Description: Imperia takes on a serene and majestic form, appearing as an  ethereal figure with a regal countenance. Her skin shimmers with a silvery glow,  reminiscent of the moon, and her eyes exude an aura of wisdom and benevolence. Imperia  is often depicted holding a staff or scepter, symbolizing her authority.    Explanation: Imperia represents the ideal of control as a force for order and harmony. She  governs the balance of power and responsibility, ensuring that order is maintained without  oppressive tyranny. Her domain is closely tied to the moon, symbolizing the cycles of time  and the influence of celestial bodies on the world.
Lexia, the Aspect of Law (Lawful Neutral)

Physical Description: Lexia embodies the embodiment of order and structure. She appears  as a figure of perfect symmetry and precision, clad in robes of intricate designs and  patterns. Her eyes radiate a keen, calculating intelligence, and she often carries a tome or  scroll containing the laws of the universe.    Explanation: Lexia personifies the strict and impartial aspects of law. She is the guardian of  rules, governance, and the balance between chaos and control. Lexia's influence extends to  the realms of magic, as she upholds the principles that govern the arcane and mystical  forces in the world.    Castia, the Aspect of Punishment (Lawful Evil)

Physical Description: Castia appears as a stern and imposing figure, draped in dark,  foreboding robes. Her eyes are sharp and judgmental, and her presence carries an air of  strict discipline and authority. She wields a scepter adorned with symbols of punishment  and consequence.    Explanation: Castia embodies the darker side of control, focusing on punishment,  retribution, and the enforcement of law through fear. She seeks to maintain control  through strict adherence to rules, and her dominion is characterized by a system of  penalties and consequences for those who transgress.
